description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held tightly together by three wide metal hoops. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain and subtle weathering, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements or frequent use. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, forming a closed container. On one side, there is a small wooden spigot or bung, indicating the barrel could be used for storing or dispensing liquids. The metal hoops are slightly tarnished, adding to the rustic, utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with metal hoops and a small side spigot, featuring a rustic, weathered look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat wooden top and two visible metal hoops, with no spigot visible.
description_view_1: Side view with a clear look at the wood grain and metal hoops, still no spigot visible.
description_view_2: Angled view revealing the small wooden spigot or bung on the right side, along with the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Another angled view with the spigot more prominently visible on the left, showing the barrel's curvature and construction.
